articles.title: NASA Answers Your Most Pressing Artemis II Questions
articles.description: The first crewed test flight under NASA’s Artemis program is underway. articles.title: Artemis 2 astronauts — now halfway to the moon — report 'burning smell' from toilet, but everything's fine
articles.description: The astronauts on NASA's Artemis 2 moon mission reported a burning smell from their Orion spacecraft toilet overnight, but Mission Control isn't overly concerned. They are over halfway to the moon today.

articles.title: It's official: NASA's Artemis 2 moon mission will break humanity's all-time distance record
articles.description: NASA's Artemis 2 moon mission will get a maximum of 252,757 miles (406,773 kilometers) from Earth on April 6, breaking the mark set by Apollo 13 in April 1970.

articles.title: 1st results from Blue Ghost lunar lander reveal how much we still don't know about the moon
articles.description: Data from Firefly Aerospace's Blue Ghost lunar lander casts doubt on the decades-old view of the moon as divided between a hotter near side and cooler far side.

articles.description: NASA’s first Artemis II science officers—Kelsey Young, Trevor Graff, and Angela Garcia—are pioneering a new era of mission operations by integrating advanced lunar science directly into Mission Control.
